title: Moroccan Lamb Kefta Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring lamb kefta cooked in a flavorful tagine with mentaiko and shio-koji cure, wrapped in lotus leaf for a unique and aromatic experience. This recipe combines the richness of lamb with the brightness of mentaiko and the depth of shio-koji cure.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, mentaiko, shio-koji, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, and coriander, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Cut the lotus leaves into large squares and blanch them in boiling water for 30 seconds to make them pliable.
Divide the lamb mixture into 4 equal portions and shape each portion into a small cylinder.
Wrap each cylinder in a lotus leaf square, tucking the edges under the kefta to form a tight package.
Place the kefta packages in a large tagine or Dutch oven and add enough water to come halfway up the sides of the pot.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the kefta for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and the lotus leaves are fragrant and slightly caramelized.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
